info.description: Privacy-focused enterprise search API. Sercha Core provides full-text and semantic search across your connected data sources.
info.title: Sercha Core API

get.description: Retrieve job execution history with filtering and pagination

parameters.description: Filter by status (pending, processing, completed, failed)
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: Maximum number of jobs to return (default: 50, max: 100)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of jobs to skip for pagination
parameters.name: offset

get.description: Compute aggregated job statistics for a time period
Retrieve pending tasks and scheduled task configurations
Retrieve detailed information about a specific job

post.description: Create tasks to reindex sources. If no source IDs are provided, all enabled sources are reindexed.

get.description: Compute search usage analytics for a time period
Retrieve recent search queries

parameters.description: Maximum number of searches to return (default: 50, max: 100)
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Compute search performance metrics for a time period
Get system-wide statistics including document counts, chunk counts, source counts, installation counts, and user counts. Useful for admin dashboards and monitoring.

post.description: Connect to a Vespa cluster and deploy the search schema (admin only). Use dev_mode=true for local development, dev_mode=false for production clusters.

get.description: Check if the Vespa cluster is healthy (admin only)
Get detailed Vespa cluster metrics including document counts, storage, query performance, and feed stats (admin only)
Get the current Vespa connection and schema status (admin only)

post.description: Authenticate with email and password to receive a JWT token
Exchange a refresh token for a new JWT token

get.description: Returns information about what features are available based on environment configuration
Get all connector connections. Connections represent authenticated connections to external data sources (OAuth tokens, API keys, etc.).

post.description: Create a new installation for non-OAuth connectors (API key, path-based). Used for connectors like localfs.

get.description: Get a connector installation by ID. Returns installation metadata without secrets.

delete.description: Delete a connector installation. Cannot delete installations that are in use by sources.

get.description: List available containers (repositories, drives, spaces, etc.) for an installation. Use this to populate a resource picker UI for selecting which containers to index.
List all sources using a specific connection. This shows which sources depend on this connection.

post.description: Test if an installation's credentials are still valid. This attempts to authenticate with the external service.

get.description: Get a document by ID with all its chunks
Get all chunks for a specific document. Chunks are the indexed segments of a document used for search.
Get the external URL for a document. This returns the original URL in the source system (e.g., GitHub, Notion, etc.).
Returns 200 if the service is up, with status of each dependency in the body

200.description: Service is up with dependency status

get.description: Get the currently authenticated user's profile

Handle the OAuth callback from an external provider. This endpoint is called by the provider after the user authorizes the application. It exchanges the authorization code for tokens and creates a connector installation, then redirects to the UI.

302.description: Redirect to UI oauth/complete page with error

post.description: Initiate an OAuth authorization flow for a provider. Returns an authorization URL to redirect the user to. The user will be redirected back to /api/v1/oauth/callback after authorizing.

parameters.description: Authorization request (provider_type optional, inferred from path)
parameters.name: request

get.description: Get a list of all available data source providers with their configuration status (admin only)
Returns the readiness status of the API (checks database and service connections)

post.description: Execute a search query across all indexed documents. Supports hybrid (BM25 + semantic), text-only, and semantic-only modes.

get.description: Get system settings (admin only)
Get AI provider configuration (admin only). Shows provider/model choice and credential availability from environment.

put.description: Update AI provider configuration (admin only). This triggers hot-reload of AI services.

get.description: Returns metadata about available AI providers and their models. Used for populating provider/model selection dropdowns in the UI.
Get the current status of AI services including embedding, LLM, and Vespa connection status

post.description: Test connectivity to configured AI providers (admin only)
Create the initial admin user. This endpoint can only be called once when no users exist.

get.description: Returns the current setup state for FTUE (First-Time User Experience) flow. This endpoint is public and does not require authentication.
Get a list of all configured data sources with sync status

post.description: Create a new data source (admin only)

get.description: Get sync states for all sources. Returns the sync status, last sync time, and statistics for each source.
Get a data source by ID

put.description: Update a source's configuration, name, or enabled status (admin only).

delete.description: Delete a data source by ID (admin only). This also removes all indexed documents from this source.

put.description: Update which containers (repos, drives, spaces) a source should index. Pass an empty array to index all available containers.

post.description: Disable a source from syncing. Disabled sources will not be included in scheduled syncs.

get.description: Get all documents indexed from a specific source with pagination support.

parameters.description: Maximum number of documents to return (default 20, max 100)
parameters.name: limit

400.description: Missing source ID or invalid parameters

post.description: Enable a source for syncing. Enabled sources will be included in scheduled syncs.

get.description: Get the current sync state and statistics for a specific source. Returns the last sync time, status, and document/chunk counts from the most recent sync operation.

post.description: Trigger a sync operation for a specific source (admin only)

get.description: Get a list of all users (admin only)
Get a user by ID (admin only)

delete.description: Delete a user by ID (admin only)

post.description: Reset a user's password (admin only). This invalidates all existing sessions for the user.

get.description: Returns the current API version

requires_base_url.description: Whether base URL is needed (e.g., Ollama)

execution_logs.description: ExecutionLogs contains log entries for this job (future enhancement)

source_name.description: SourceName is the name of the source (if applicable)

task.description: Task is the main task information

has_more.description: HasMore indicates if there are more jobs beyond the current page

jobs.description: Jobs is the list of historical job executions

total_count.description: TotalCount is the total number of jobs matching the query

average_duration_ms.description: AverageDuration is the average job duration in milliseconds

completed_jobs.description: CompletedJobs is the number of successfully completed jobs

failed_jobs.description: FailedJobs is the number of failed jobs

jobs_by_type.description: JobsByType shows the breakdown by task type

pending_jobs.description: PendingJobs is the number of jobs waiting to be processed

period.description: Period is the time range for these statistics

processing_jobs.description: ProcessingJobs is the number of jobs currently being processed

success_rate.description: SuccessRate is the percentage of successful jobs (0-100)

total_retries.description: TotalRetries is the total number of retry attempts

error.description: Error is the error message from this attempt

timestamp.description: Timestamp is when this attempt occurred

enabled.description: Enabled indicates if the schedule is active

id.description: ID is the unique identifier for this scheduled task

interval.description: Interval is how often to run the task

last_error.description: LastError contains the last error if the scheduled task failed

last_run.description: LastRun is when the task was last triggered

name.description: Name is a human-readable name for the task

next_run.description: NextRun is when the task should next be triggered

team_id.description: TeamID is the team this schedule belongs to

type.description: Type is the task type to create when triggered

average_duration_ms.description: AverageDuration is the average search duration in milliseconds

average_results.description: AverageResults is the average number of results per search

period.description: Period is the time range for these analytics

searches_by_mode.description: SearchesByMode shows the breakdown by search mode

top_queries.description: TopQueries are the most common search queries

total_searches.description: TotalSearches is the total number of searches in the period

unique_users.description: UniqueUsers is the number of unique users who searched

fast_searches.description: FastSearches is the number of searches under 100ms

medium_searches.description: MediumSearches is the number of searches 100ms-500ms

p50_duration_ms.description: P50Duration is the 50th percentile search duration in milliseconds

p95_duration_ms.description: P95Duration is the 95th percentile search duration in milliseconds

p99_duration_ms.description: P99Duration is the 99th percentile search duration in milliseconds

period.description: Period is the time range for these metrics

slow_searches.description: SlowSearches is the number of searches over 500ms

zero_result_searches.description: ZeroResultSearches is the number of searches that returned no results

created_at.description: CreatedAt is when the search was performed

duration.description: Duration is how long the search took to execute (in nanoseconds)

has_filters.description: Filters are the additional filters applied

id.description: ID is the unique identifier for this search query log entry

mode.description: Mode is the search mode used (hybrid, text, semantic)

query.description: Query is the search text that was submitted

result_count.description: ResultCount is the number of results returned

source_ids.description: SourceIDs are the source IDs that were filtered (if any)

team_id.description: TeamID is the team that performed the search

user_id.description: UserID is the user who performed the search

connection_id.description: ConnectionID references the connector connection for authentication

containers.description:
Containers lists the containers to index (typed)
Empty means index all accessible containers
Examples: repos for GitHub, channels for Slack, folders for Drive

attempts.description: Attempts is how many times this task has been attempted

completed_at.description: CompletedAt is when processing finished (nil if not complete)

created_at.description: CreatedAt is when the task was enqueued

error.description: Error contains the last error message if failed

id.description: ID is the unique identifier for this task

max_attempts.description: MaxAttempts is the maximum retry count before giving up

priority.description:
Priority determines processing order (higher = more urgent)
Default is 0, range is -100 to 100

scheduled_for.description: ScheduledFor is when the task should be processed (for delayed tasks)

started_at.description: StartedAt is when processing began (nil if not started)

status.description: Status is the current state of the task

team_id.description: TeamID is the team this task belongs to

type.description: Type identifies what kind of task this is

updated_at.description: UpdatedAt is when the task was last modified

next_scheduled_run.description: NextScheduledRun is when the next scheduled task will run

pending_tasks.description: PendingTasks are tasks ready to be processed now

services_xml.description: Raw XML content (for persistence and future reference)

description.description: Description is an optional description of the container.

id.description:
ID is the provider-specific identifier.
Format varies by provider:
  - GitHub: "owner/repo"
  - Google Drive: drive ID
  - Confluence: space key

name.description: Name is the human-readable display name.

type.description:
Type identifies the container type.
Examples: "repository", "shared_drive", "space", "project", "channel"

embedding.description: Embedding lists providers available for embedding service

llm.description: LLM lists providers available for LLM service

github_com_custodia-labs_sercha-core_int.description: Request to start OAuth authorization flow

installation_name.description:
InstallationName is an optional name for the installation.
If not provided, defaults to "{Provider} ({AccountID})"

provider_type.description: ProviderType is the OAuth provider (github, slack, notion, etc.)

return_context.description:
ReturnContext indicates where to redirect after OAuth completes.
Values: "setup" (FTUE), "admin-sources" (add source wizard), or empty for default.

github_com_custodia-labs_sercha-core_int.description: Response containing the OAuth authorization URL

authorization_url.description: AuthorizationURL is the URL to redirect the user to for authorization.

expires_at.description: ExpiresAt is when the authorization state expires (typically 10 minutes).

state.description:
State is the CSRF token that will be returned in the callback.
This is provided for reference - the frontend should not need to track it.

github_com_custodia-labs_sercha-core_int.description: Information about what features are enabled via environment configuration

ai_providers.description: AIProviders lists AI providers available for embedding and LLM

limits.description: Limits defines operational boundaries from environment configuration

oauth_providers.description: OAuthProviders lists OAuth providers configured via environment variables

dev_mode.description:
DevMode controls deployment behavior:
- true: Deploy our full app package (services.xml + schema) - for local development
- false: Fetch existing app package, add our schema, redeploy - for production clusters

endpoint.description: Endpoint is the Vespa cluster endpoint (optional, uses stored/default if empty)

github_com_custodia-labs_sercha-core_int.description: Request to create a connection for API key or path-based connectors

api_key.description: APIKey is the authentication credential (or path for localfs).

name.description: Name is a human-readable name for the connection.

provider_type.description: ProviderType is the data source provider.

semantic_search.description: SemanticSearch indicates if semantic search is available (requires embedding service)

vector_indexing.description: VectorIndexing indicates if vector indexing is available (requires Vespa with embeddings)

max_results_per_page.description: MaxResultsPerPage is the maximum results per page

max_workers.description: MaxWorkers is the maximum number of sync workers

sync_max_interval.description: SyncMaxInterval is the maximum sync interval in minutes

sync_min_interval.description: SyncMinInterval is the minimum sync interval in minutes

github_com_custodia-labs_sercha-core_int.description: Paginated list of containers available for indexing

containers.description: Containers is the list of available containers (repos, drives, spaces, etc.)

has_more.description: HasMore indicates if there are more containers to fetch.

next_cursor.description: NextCursor is the cursor for the next page, empty if no more pages.

priority.description: Priority sets the task priority (-100 to 100)

source_ids.description:
SourceIDs optionally specifies which sources to reindex
If empty, all enabled sources are reindexed

can_upgrade.description: CanUpgrade indicates if schema can be upgraded to hybrid

cluster_info.description: ClusterInfo contains parsed information about the Vespa cluster (production mode only)

connected.description: Connected indicates if Vespa is reachable and schema is deployed

dev_mode.description: DevMode indicates if we deployed full app package (true) or just added schema (false)

embedding_dim.description: EmbeddingDim is the embedding dimension if embeddings are enabled

embedding_provider.description: EmbeddingProvider is the provider used for embeddings

embeddings_enabled.description: EmbeddingsEnabled indicates if hybrid search is available

endpoint.description: Endpoint is the Vespa cluster endpoint

healthy.description: Healthy indicates if the Vespa cluster is healthy

indexed_chunks.description: IndexedChunks is the number of chunks currently indexed in Vespa

reindex_required.description: ReindexRequired indicates if documents need reindexing

schema_mode.description: SchemaMode indicates the deployed schema type (bm25, hybrid)

schema_version.description: SchemaVersion is the deployed schema version

internal_adapters_driving_http.AdminStat.description: System-wide statistics for the admin dashboard

internal_adapters_driving_http.SourceDoc.description: Paginated list of documents belonging to a source

internal_adapters_driving_http.TriggerRe.description: Response containing task IDs created for reindex operation

internal_adapters_driving_http.UpdateCon.description: Request to update which containers a source should index

containers.description:
Containers is the list of containers to index.
Empty list means index all available containers.

has_api_key.description: True if credentials are configured via environment

is_configured.description: True if provider and model are selected
